revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Iwase-Senzuka-Kofungruppe]][[en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:岩橋千塚古墳群]][[zh:岩橋千塚古墳群]]
{{Short description|Kofuns in Wakayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster | native_name = 岩橋千塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Iwase-senzuka-kofungun-1.JPG|image= File:Iwase-senzuka-kofungun-1.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|13|29.8|N|135|13|40.6|E|region:JP-30|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.2236|N|135.2301|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt= Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of =; {{ill|Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|qid=Q11373771|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Q55452706|qid=Q55452706|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22}}; {{ill|Q55529436|qid=Q55529436|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35}}; {{ill|Shōgunzuka Kofun|qid=Q55523132|1=Shōgunzuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Tennozuka Kofun|qid=Q48763062|1=Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}}; {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|qid=Q11417470|1=Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore}}| length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at sie) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}; {{ill|Special Historic Site|qid=Q26764449|1=Special Historic Site}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:天王塚古墳_(和歌山市)_出土品-1.JPG|thumb|Excavated items from the [[:ja: 天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|Tennozuka Kofun]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' |岩橋千塚古墳群|Iwase-Senzuka kofun gun}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=岩橋千塚古墳群 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202376|access-date=2021-07-24|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref> is a cluster of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s that is located in the {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=岩橋千塚古墳群の概要|url=https://www.kiifudoki.wakayama-c.ed.jp/iwase_tomb/iwase_tomb.htm|access-date=2021-07-24|website=www.kiifudoki.wakayama-c.ed.jp}}</ref> One of the largest concentrations of Kofuns in Japan, it consist of over 900 tumuli.<ref>{{Cite web|last=国指定史跡ガイド,百科事典マイペディア|title=岩橋千塚古墳群とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%A9%E6%A9%8B%E5%8D%83%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-207534|access-date=2021-07-24|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=岩橋千塚とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%A9%E6%A9%8B%E5%8D%83%E5%A1%9A-32672|access-date=2021-07-24|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> It is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1931, upgraded to a Special National Historic Site in 1952, and the area under designation expanded in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2070|access-date=2021-07-24|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located in the eastern portion of the city of Wakayama,on an east-west extension of the Ryumon Mountains, and west of Yada Pass. It consists of a group of ancient [[Kofun]]s related to the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}, and consists of approximately 900 [[kofun]], making it one of the largest sites in Japan. These tumuli are divided between the Hanayama branch in the Narugami district in the north and the Ibe Hachiman Kofun in the Okazaki district in the south. In the western portion of the site are the [[Hinokuma Shrine]] and Kunikakasu Shrine, which enshrine the spirits of the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]''. The ''kofun'' consist mostly of {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and {{ill|Houfun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Houfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), with only one percent as {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is believed that these tumuli were all constructed from around the middleof the 4th century, until the proclamation of the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]] in the middleof the 7th century. With regards to the burial facilities, the earliest ''kofun'' had clay-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with box-type sarcophagus. This evolved into {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|qid=Q15315|lt=chlorite schist|1=Schist}}-lined stone chambers for later burials. The schist was separated into slabs, which were stacked to form the walls, into which several stone beams were embedded for structural integrity, and the ceiling was covered with large stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavations began in 1906, and have been ongoing intermittently to then present day. Many of the finds from the site are on display at the Kiifudoki-no-oka Museum of History.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 岩橋千塚古墳群前山A13号墳.JPG|Maeyama Kofun A13 Corridor from the entrance to the burial chamber 岩橋千塚古墳群前山A46号墳.JPG|Maeyama Kofun A46 Burial chamber 岩橋千塚古墳群前山A56号墳.JPG|Maeyama Kofun A56 Entrance area to the stone chamber 岩橋千塚古墳群前山A100号墳.JPG|Maeyama Kofun A100 Sarcophagus made from stone slabs 岩橋千塚古墳群前山A111号墳.JPG|Maeyama Kofun A111 Shaft-like stone chamber Shogunzuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu-2.jpg|Maeyama Kofun B53, Shōgun-zuka Kofun Shogunzuka Kofun (Wakayama), genshitsu-1.jpg|Maeyama Kofun B53, Stone chamber of the Shogunzsuka Kofun Chijizuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Maeyama Kofun B67, Chijizuka Kofun Gunchozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Maeyama Kofun B112, Gunchōzuka Kofun Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), genshitsu-2.jpg|Stone chamber of the Tennōzuka Kofun Hanayama Kofun Group-6, funkyu.jpg|Hanayama Kofun 6 Kofun Hanayama Kofun Group-6, sekishitsu.jpg|Hanayama Kofun 6 Stone chamber Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Inbe Maeyama Kofun B10, Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|qid=Q6573290|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)}}
